item.title: Senate moving forward with reconciliation bill, dropping ballroom funds
item.description: Senate Republicans are preparing to move forward with a package to fund the Department of Homeland Security's immigration agencies Wednesday.

item.title: Could Intel's chips save the U.S. economy?
item.description: Patrick McGee, a contributing writer for The Free Press, joins CBS News 24/7 to discuss his latest column about how Intel's semiconductor chips could be the key to bolstering the U.S. economy. Paramount Skydance owns The Free Press and CBS News.

item.title: Rubio spars with Rep. Lieu over claims of Trump sleeping at meetings, apparent cognitive decline
item.description: California Democratic Rep. Ted Lieu grilled State Secretary Marco Rubio about President Trump's health, showing video at a House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing of past meetings in which Mr. Trump appears to be asleep. Lieu also asked Rubio about the president's health after his repeated medical visits.

item.title: Iran calls deadly attack on Kuwait retaliation for latest U.S. strikes
item.description: After Trump insisted talks with Iran were "going on continuously," Iran fired a deadly missile and drone salvo at Kuwait, claiming retaliation for new U.S. strikes.

item.title: Karen Bass to advance in LA mayor's race as Pratt and Raman vie for final spot
item.description: CBS News projects that incumbent Mayor Karen Bass will advance to the November election, while her opponents, Councilmember Nithya Raman and political newcomer Spencer Pratt, compete for the final spot.

item.title: Knicks take on Spurs in Game 1 of legendary NBA Finals rematch
item.description: The New York Knicks are set to face the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the NBA Finals, in a long-awaited rematch of the 1999 Finals. CBS News' Jason Allen has more.

item.title: Watch: Rubio says he won't talk about Israel's nuclear capacities in public hearing
item.description: Texas Democratic Rep. Joaquin Castro asked State Secretary Marco Rubio to expand on Israel's nuclear capacities during a House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing on Thursday, as the Iran war rages on and as Israel continues to strike Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: El Niño could bring more widespread coral bleaching, NOAA warns
item.description: The expected arrival of El Niño this summer could trigger another mass coral bleaching event, which would be the fifth on record, researchers said.

item.title: Trump touts Iran progress as Tehran fires at troops in Kuwait
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday that Iran has agreed not to have a nuclear weapon. Meanwhile, Tehran fired at U.S. troops in Kuwait. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Weijia Jiang have more.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil terminal, warship as strikes ramp up between the countries
item.description: Ukraine struck a Russian oil terminal and a warship, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Wednesday, as the countries ramp up attacks against each other. CBS News' Aidan Stretch has more.

item.title: Hostage situation at California bank ends after suspect was shot and killed, police say
item.description: The FBI shot and killed a person suspected of holding people hostage at a bank in Bakersfield, California, police said, adding that all hostages are safe. CBS News' Jonathan Vigliotti has the latest.

item.title: Rubio on Bill Pulte's intelligence credentials after Trump's stunning appointment
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio testified before the House Foreign Affairs Committee on Wednesday, where he was asked about Bill Pulte's intelligence credentials after President Trump appointed him to be the acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Social Security checks could be cut by $500 a month in 2032, report finds
item.description: Beneficiaries would continue receiving payments if Social Security's trust fund is depleted, but checks could shrink by about 24%, according to a new report.

item.title: Watch: Lawmaker presses Rubio on impacts of Iran war and Trump's stock purchases linked to conflict
item.description: New York Democratic Rep. Gregory Meeks asked Secretary of State Marco Rubio about his warnings to President Trump before the Iran war erupted, and pressed him on Mr. Trump's apparent stock investments that may be linked to companies involved in U.S. military infrastructure. Texas GOP Rep. Michael McCaul then allowed Rubio to use his time during the House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing to respond to Meeks' questions.

item.title: Can nursing home debt lead to Social Security garnishment?
item.description: Wondering if an unpaid nursing home bill will impact your Social Security check? Here's what federal law allows.

item.title: About Us
item.description: "60 Minutes" airs Sundays at 7 p.m. ET/PT

item.title: California bank hostage situation ends after suspect shot dead
item.description: The suspect was pronounced dead at the scene early Wednesday morning, the Bakersfield Police Department said.

item.title: Todd Blanche defends the DOJ's apparently defunct "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche told the House Appropriations Committee Tuesday that the Justice Department was no longer moving forward with its "anti-weaponization" fund, but he defended its creation. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: GOP Rep. Kean returning to in-person work in "matter of weeks" after absence
item.description: The New Jersey congressman last voted on March 5.

item.title: Filmmaker and Knicks fan Spike Lee talks NBA Finals: "NYC is on fire"
item.description: The NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks get underway Wednesday. Oscar winning filmmaker Spike Lee speaks to "CBS Mornings" about his decades of being a Knicks fan, why he's confident in the team and the NBA Finals.

item.title: Lynette Hooker's Bahamas disappearance being investigated as possible murder, official says
item.description: Lynette Hooker's disappearance is being investigated as a possible murder, an official says, after her husband reported her missing during a sailing trip in the Bahamas. CBS News' Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: California governor's race narrows as Hilton, Becerra take early lead
item.description: Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer have emerged as the leading contenders to advance to November's general election, according to early counts.

item.title: Medical experts say Ebola response is not enough as virus spreads
item.description: CBS News' Ramy Inocencio spoke to Tedros Ghebreyesus, the head of the World Health Organization, about the response to a deadly Ebola outbreak in Africa, expressing concern about the current measures.

item.title: Scientists find yeast in frozen mummy's guts, use it to make bread
item.description: More than 5,300 years ago, Oetzi the Iceman was strolling through the Alps on the border of Austria and Italy when he was killed by an arrow in the back.

item.title: Zach Lahn wins Iowa GOP governor primary, upsetting Trump-backed rival
item.description: MAHA-aligned Republican Zach Lahn will face Democrat Rob Sand in this year's race for Iowa governor, setting up what could be an unusually competitive contest in a red-leaning state.

item.title: Iowa GOP Senate candidate Hinson: Prolonged Iran war could be "political liability"
item.description: Iowa GOP Senate candidate Rep. Ashley Hinson acknowledged the U.S.-Iran conflict could become a "political liability" if it stretches beyond the next few weeks, according to audio obtained by CBS News.

item.title: Upcoming billing change could make pregnancy pricier
item.description: Come January, pregnancy care physician billing codes will change from a bundled system to an à la carte one.

item.title: Trump admin. floats tariffs on 60 trading partners after forced labor probe
item.description: The Trump administration has unveiled proposed tariffs of 10% or more on dozens of countries accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, including some of the U.S.'s largest trading partners.

item.title: What travelers should know about Europe's new Entry/Exit System
item.description: Travel experts say to be prepared for potential disruptions as countries implement the new Entry/Exit System now in place across the EU and other countries.

item.title: WHO massively scales back number of suspected Ebola cases in Congo
item.description: While 330 Ebola infections are confirmed in central Africa and huge challenges remain, hundreds more suspected cases "have been cleared out," the WHO says.
